# Copilot instructions for this repository

## Build, test, and lint

Run all commands from the repository root with `pnpm` (the repo is pinned to `pnpm@9.15.4` and Node `>=18`).

```bash
pnpm start          # local Docusaurus dev server
pnpm build          # production build
pnpm typecheck      # TypeScript check
pnpm exec eslint .  # lint; there is no dedicated lint script in package.json
```

There is currently **no automated test runner configured** in `package.json`, so there is no single-test command to reuse.

Current command baseline in this repo:

- `pnpm build` succeeds
- `pnpm exec eslint .` currently reports stylistic errors in `sidebars.ts`
- `pnpm typecheck` currently fails in both dependency typings and local files (`src/components/Tooltip/index.tsx`, `src/theme/CodeBlock/Content/String.tsx`)

## High-level architecture

This is a **Docusaurus 3** personal site with custom React/TypeScript theme overrides, not just a stock docs/blog setup.

- `docusaurus.config.ts` is the main integration point. It enables docs, disables the preset blog, then re-adds blog support through the local plugin at `src/plugin/plugin-content-blog/index.js`. It also wires Algolia search, Giscus comments, Vercel analytics, Tailwind, image zoom, and custom head/script injection.
- Content comes from three places:
  - `docs/` for documentation pages, with `sidebars.ts` autogenerated from the filesystem
  - `blog/` for blog posts and `blog/authors.yml` for author metadata
  - `data/*.ts(x)` for non-Markdown content that powers site features such as the project showcase and social links
- The local blog plugin in `src/plugin/plugin-content-blog/index.js` is important: it sorts posts by `frontMatter.sticky`, rewrites paginated blog list items after sorting, and exposes recent post counts/tag counts through `setGlobalData()`. Components like `src/components/UserCard/index.tsx` depend on that global plugin data.
- Most of the site UI is customized through swizzled Docusaurus theme components under `src/theme/`. The blog list, blog post page, archive, sidebar, code block, navbar, TOC, paginator, and layout are all overridden there.
- Top-level pages live in `src/pages/`. The homepage (`src/pages/index.tsx`) is a custom landing page built from `src/components/landing/*`; the project showcase page reads typed data from `data/projects.tsx`.

## Key conventions

- Prefer editing **content/data at the source**:
  - blog content in `blog/*.md`
  - docs content in `docs/*.md`
  - project/social metadata in `data/projects.tsx` and `data/social.ts`
  Do not scatter that data across components.
- Blog behavior is driven by frontmatter conventions used across the custom plugin and theme overrides. In addition to normal Docusaurus blog fields, this repo uses:
  - `sticky` to pin/reorder posts
  - `hide_table_of_contents`
  - `toc_min_heading_level`
  - `toc_max_heading_level`
  - `hide_comment`
  - `authors` values that must match keys in `blog/authors.yml`
  - `<!-- truncate -->` for list excerpts
- When touching `src/theme/*` blog components, preserve Docusaurus wrappers/providers such as `BlogPostProvider`, `HtmlClassNameProvider`, `ThemeClassNames`, and theme prop shapes. Many overrides rely on Docusaurus context rather than standalone props.
- Tailwind is layered on top of Docusaurus styling rather than replacing it:
  - use the `cn()` helper from `src/lib/utils.ts` for class composition
  - prefer Tailwind classes that reference Docusaurus CSS variables (`bg-background`, `text-text`, `border`, etc.)
  - do not enable Tailwind preflight; it is intentionally disabled in `tailwind.config.ts`
- Formatting/linting is ESLint-driven, not Prettier-driven. `.vscode/settings.json` disables Prettier and expects ESLint fixes instead.
- Imports commonly use the Docusaurus `@site/...` alias for repo-root paths; follow that pattern instead of deep relative imports when possible.
